meta.source: Captured from Book 2 manuscript chapters 1-5 (~16,600 words) and Book 1 'Riftmarked' chapter 1.5 sample
meta.note: Descriptive, not prescriptive. When drafting, match these patterns; when in doubt, imitate the nearest written scene.

banned_list.source: planning/source-banned-list.md — the author's BANNED: THE DEFINITIVE GUIDE. HARD CONSTRAINT: audit every drafted scene against it before saving.
highest_frequency_traps: 'something' + verb as interiority placeholder — name what is actually happening
anthropomorphized silence (stretches/hangs/sits/belongs) — show who breaks it and what it costs
'not X, but Y' hedges and 'isn't quite' reactions — commit
triple-beat noun/fragment lists and echo-line parallel sentences
'for a (long) moment' / 'a pause, then' beat placeholders
narrated cognition: filed/noted/registered/catalogued — cut every instance
stock physical tells: jaw works/tightens, breath catches, knuckles whiten, goes very still
precision/control cluster: surgical, practiced ease, methodical, calculated
texture defaults: steel/iron/velvet/silk; temperature metaphors for emotion
impact similes (like a blow/punch) and stone-in-water ripples
adverb dialogue tags (softly, quietly, finally); voice drops/hardens
articulation by proxy: 'a look that said', 'didn't need words'
'commanded the room' presence cliches; gravitational attraction metaphors
banned_list.workflow: Draft, then grep the new text against the quick-scan table in the source doc before committing. The author's existing prose is exempt from retroactive edits unless they ask.

pov_and_structure.pov: Close third person, single POV per chapter, rotating (Brunor, Elaine, Phoebe, Frederick planned)
pov_and_structure.dateline_block: Bracketed lines open each chapter/section: [date, e.g. 8th of Ashenfall, 982 A.M.] [Day of X | Season — Month (Deity)] [Time span | Place, Region, Realm]
pov_and_structure.epigraph: Each chapter opens with a one-line first-person italic interior question/statement from the POV character (e.g. 'As the world gets torn asunder, what will my faith demand of me?')
pov_and_structure.scene_breaks: '***' or '-----' between scenes; datelines reappear for major time/location shifts

prose_texture.sentences: Long, sensory, clause-stacked description alternating with short declarative punches ('Cold kept a man true.'). Fragments used for tactical beats: 'Left treeline, right treeline. Flank. Tracks. Listening.'
prose_texture.interiority: Free indirect discourse dominant; direct thought in italics, sparingly, often one line ('He grounded me... He put me in an enclosure.')
prose_texture.sensory_priority: Smell and sound lead scene-setting (damp pine, tanned leather, hot wax, the salty mineral tang of the Aldren); temperature and rain as persistent physical presence
prose_texture.punctuation: Heavy, confident em-dash use for interruption and apposition; ellipses rare, for trailing speech
prose_texture.spelling_note: COPYEDIT FLAG: mixed British/American spellings in manuscript (colour/color, recognised/recognized) - author to pick one

combat_writing.approach: Technical and legible: named fencing concepts (measure, tempo, bind, riposte, parry, invitation) filtered through POV expertise; reads opponents via footwork and weight
combat_writing.rhythm: Exchanges in short paragraphs; sensory cost emphasized (forearms singing, split lip 'like mashed cherries'); no gore for its own sake
combat_writing.signature: Fights are characterization - Damien fights 'as if correcting a pupil'; Brunor hunts the flaw in the pattern; psychological warfare mid-duel

dialogue.general: Banter-forward, distinct idiolects, action beats instead of said-bookisms; arguments escalate in long alternating volleys
dialogue.brunor: Terse, flat declaratives; deflects; goes silent under emotion; dry cold humor ('It's even harder now.')
dialogue.elaine: Surgical, escalating; rhetorical questions as weapons; rants that run their course and pivot to absurd specifics (six carps for stabling); 'Unbecoming.' as verdict
dialogue.frederick: Self-dramatizing jocularity ('Drinking alone. Like a hound.'); counts things; blunt honesty when it matters ('Two ropes... I count what's there.')
dialogue.damien: Courtly cruelty, theatrical, speaks to the crowd not the opponent; surgical insults dressed as observation
dialogue.tella: Rough warmth; imperatives; 'child'; teaching metaphors (move with the grain, direct the blade with your soul)
dialogue.west_book1: Clinical detachment, dry irony, unbothered authority ('In this room, I outrank the King himself.')
dialogue.phoebe: Quick, indignant, young; challenges authority then yields with conditions

riftmark_rendering.rule: Emotions arrive as readable objects with texture and weight, in slices; the mark 'catches', 'reads', 'gives nothing'; absence/void is more alarming than intensity
riftmark_rendering.examples: 'the hawkers' practiced cheer lay thin over something wound too tight underneath'; 'an old guilt sat packed and quiet under a face doing nothing at all'; 'Calm has a shape. There wasn't a single emotion I could read.'
riftmark_rendering.nullbrand_interaction: Proximity to Brunor/Nullbrand mutes the mark ('Nullbrand's suffocating aura', 'the hush slid off her'); distance wakes it

recurring_motifs: Eyes catching/holding light (Johanna's amber vs Elaine's 'permafrost trapping a flame in time')
Monsters / 'we breed what we are' / who gets to say what a monster is
Promises, names, legacy ('Leave something worth remembering behind')
Rain as pressure and concealment; the two moons as Brunor's 'oldest companions'
Ravens and feathers (Elaine's cloak); matched blackness (hair, horse, cloak, sword)
Counting as coping (Elaine's exits and eyes; Frederick counting what's there)

profanity_and_register.profanity: Sparing but hard when used ('ratshite', 'worthless fucking company', 'Fuck off'); coarser in Frederick/Mikah/Damien registers; Elaine swears when control slips
profanity_and_register.content: Adult; violence with consequences; implied sexual violence handled at a distance (Damien's taunt about Alessa cut off mid-sentence)

worldbuilding_delivery.rule: Facts arrive through use and opinion, never exposition dumps - castles as 'receipts', the council through Brunor's contempt, history through what locals call a square (the Crimson Cage)
worldbuilding_delivery.names: Hard-consonant Germanic/Celtic-flavored (Caer Seryth, Uthred Halvyr, Vespine's Cradle) beside softer romance names (Elaine, Damien, Celisia)
